scope.summary: Add frame-range parameters (start_frame: u32, end_frame: u32) to the glTF and Alembic export paths so operators and models can export a named sub-range of the cached animation, with optional marker-name resolution for start/end anchors.
acceptance_criteria: glTF export (MT-249) accepts optional start_frame and end_frame; when provided, only samples in [start_frame, end_frame] inclusive are written to the animation clips.
Alembic export (MT-252) accepts the same range parameters and writes only the specified frame window.
OBJ-sequence export (MT-251) accepts the same range parameters and writes only files for frames in the window.
Range endpoints may be specified as either frame indices or marker names (resolved via the proposed timeline markers MT).
The export manifest (MT-320) records the exported frame range in its metadata.
scope.risk_if_missed: Every export writes the full cached animation. Hero clip extraction requires external video/3D editing tools. MD 2026.0 users expect named-range export; without it the Tailor export API is demonstrably less capable than the tool it replaces for animation production pipelines.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
